titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert Davies Papers on Hanson Baldwin
Davies, Robert papers on Hanson Baldwin
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michael Lotstein

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday August 14, 2020 at 08:27

did.unittitle: Robert Davies papers on Hanson Baldwin
did.abstract: The Robert Davies papers on Hanson Baldwin contain correspondence, Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) files, interview transcripts and audiocassettes relating to Davies's book: Baldwin of the Times: Hanson W. Baldwin, a Military Journalist's Life, 1903-1991 .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Robert Davies papers on Hanson Baldwin contain correspondence, Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) files, interview transcripts and audiocassettes relating to Davies's book: Baldwin of the Times: Hanson W. Baldwin, a Military Journalist's Life, 1903-1991 .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged by topic.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert Davies Papers on Hanson Baldwin (MS 1984) Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection whether such works are at Yale or physically located elsewhere in the world. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Bruce Davies taught at Minnesota State University Moorhead for thirty-two years, retiring in 1998. He is the author of Baldwin of the Times: Hanson W. Baldwin, a Military Journalist's Life, 1903-1991 and Peacefully Working to Conquer the World: Singer Sewing Machines in Foreign Markets, 1854-1920 .

did.unittitle: Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) records on Hanson Baldwin
Interviews with Hanson Baldwin by John T. Mason, Jr.
Interview with Hanson Baldwin and his wife, Helen Bruce Baldwin on 5 August 1987
Media influence, tapes I and II, 7 September 1987
Baldwin tape of June 1989 in reply to Davies' 25 April letter

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

odd.head: General
odd.p: The "Recollections" and "Media Influences" recordings are dubs of originals which were returned to Hanson Baldwin. There are multiple copies of some portions, which are arranged in various sequences.

did.unittitle: Response to questions in Robert Davies' 25 April 1989 letter
Potter, Mrs. Barbara [Baldwin] -- at her home in Woodbury, Connecticut
Crabtree, Elizabeth Baldwin -- at her home in Ridgewood, New Jersey
